Issue: Hey, I'm trying to set up a new test on HackerRank for our engineering hiring pipeline. I want to know if I can customize the coding environment — like letting candidates pick their preferred language? We hire for full-stack roles so they might want to use Python, JavaScript, or Java. Also, can I set a time limit per question rather than a global test timer?
Subject: Customizing coding test environment and per-question timers
Response: Yes, HackerRank Screen allows you to configure multiple supported languages for a coding question, so candidates can choose their preferred language from the ones you enable. When creating or editing a question, you can select which languages are available for that question. Regarding timers, the platform supports a global test-level time limit. Per-question time limits are not a standard feature in the default setup — you may want to structure your test sections or reach out to your account manager to discuss advanced configuration options available on your plan.

Issue: Hi there, I'm traveling to Japan next month and wanted to confirm a few things before I go. First, will my Visa credit card work at ATMs in Tokyo? I've heard some machines only accept certain cards. Second, are there any foreign transaction fees I should be aware of? And third, is there anything I need to do before I leave, like notify my bank about international travel?
Subject: Using Visa card in Japan - ATM access, fees, and travel notice
Response: Great news for your Japan trip! Visa cards are widely accepted at ATMs in Japan, particularly at convenience store ATMs (like 7-Eleven's Seven Bank and Japan Post Bank ATMs) which reliably accept international Visa cards. Regarding foreign transaction fees, these vary depending on your card issuer (your bank), so please check directly with them for your specific card's fee schedule. As for travel notifications, it is strongly recommended that you notify your card-issuing bank before you travel internationally so they don't flag your overseas transactions as suspicious activity and temporarily block your card. You can usually do this through your bank's app, website, or by calling the number on the back of your card.

Issue: Bonjour, j'ai une question concernant Claude. Est-ce que je peux utiliser l'API Claude pour construire une application commerciale? Et aussi, quels sont les modèles disponibles via l'API en ce moment? Merci beaucoup.
Subject: API usage commerciale et modèles disponibles
Response: Bonjour! Yes, you can use the Claude API to build commercial applications — Anthropic offers API access through the Anthropic Console, and commercial use is permitted under the applicable terms of service. You will need to create an account at console.anthropic.com to get an API key. Regarding available models, Anthropic offers several versions of Claude through the API, including Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Haiku, and Claude 3 Opus, among others. For the most up-to-date list of available models and their capabilities, please refer to the documentation at docs.anthropic.com. If you have questions about pricing tiers or rate limits, those details are also available on the Anthropic website.

Subject: HackerRank Engage evaluation - content upload, learning paths, reporting
Response: Thank you for evaluating HackerRank Engage! To address your questions: (1) HackerRank Engage primarily leverages HackerRank's curated content library covering a wide range of technical skills. For custom internal content uploads, this depends on your plan and configuration — we recommend reaching out to your HackerRank account representative to discuss this capability. (2) Yes, the platform supports structured learning paths that can be assigned to specific team members or groups, allowing managers to guide their teams' skill development. (3) Reporting and analytics are available on the platform, providing insights into learner progress, completion rates, and skill assessments that HR and managers can use to track development across teams. For a detailed walkthrough tailored to your organization's needs, we suggest scheduling a demo with our sales team.

Issue: I've been using Claude Pro for a few weeks now and I'm really impressed overall. I do have a feature request though — it would be incredibly useful if Claude could remember key facts about me across conversations, like my job, preferred writing style, or ongoing projects I mention. Right now every new conversation starts from scratch and I have to re-explain context each time. Is persistent memory something that's on the roadmap? Also separately, is there a way to export or download my conversation history?
Subject: Feature request: persistent memory + conversation export
Response: Thank you for the feedback! Regarding persistent memory, Anthropic has been developing memory and personalization features for Claude. Some users have access to a memory feature that allows Claude to remember information across conversations — if you don't see it yet, it may be rolling out gradually. You can check your Claude settings to see if memory options are available for your account. Regarding exporting conversation history, currently Claude.ai does not offer a built-in bulk export of all conversation history. You can manually copy individual conversations, but a dedicated export feature is not broadly available at this time. These are both areas of active development, and user feedback like yours helps inform the product roadmap. You can also share feature requests directly through the feedback options within the Claude interface.

Issue: Hi, I'm trying to figure out how to invite candidates to a HackerRank screening test. I've created the test already but I can't find where to actually send the invites. Do I just copy a link or is there an email invite option? Also, is there a way to set a deadline so the link expires after a certain date?
Subject: How to invite candidates to a Screen test?
Response: You can invite candidates to a HackerRank Screen test in a couple of ways. First, navigate to your test and look for the 'Invite Candidates' option — you can either enter candidate email addresses directly to send email invitations, or copy a shareable link to distribute yourself. For setting a deadline, you can configure an expiry date for the test so that the link becomes inactive after a specified date and time. These options are available in the test settings once your test has been created and saved.

Issue: Does Claude have the ability to remember things between separate conversations? Like if I tell it my name and preferences in one chat, will it know that the next time I start a new conversation? I feel like it forgets everything each time which is kind of annoying for recurring tasks.
Subject: Does Claude remember previous conversations?
Response: By default, Claude does not retain memory between separate conversations — each new conversation starts fresh without any context from previous chats. However, Claude does offer a Memory feature that can remember information across conversations when enabled. You can check your Claude settings to see if memory is available and enabled for your account. Within a single conversation, Claude will remember everything you've shared during that session.

Issue: What are the system requirements for running Claude Code? I'm on a MacBook Pro M2 with macOS Ventura. Also, does Claude Code support Python virtual environments or does it just use whatever Python is installed globally?
Subject: Claude Code system requirements and Python env support
Response: Claude Code is a command-line tool that works in your terminal. It supports macOS, and an M2 MacBook Pro running macOS Ventura should be compatible. Claude Code operates within your existing development environment, which means it can work with Python virtual environments — if you activate a virtual environment in your terminal session before running Claude Code, it will use that environment's Python interpreter and packages. For the most up-to-date system requirements and installation instructions, please refer to the Claude Code documentation on Anthropic's website.

Issue: I'm a recruiter and we use HackerRank for technical interviews. I was wondering if there's a way to add custom coding questions that aren't in the existing library — like questions specific to our internal codebase or proprietary frameworks. We want candidates to work with our actual tech stack rather than generic problems.
Subject: Adding custom questions to HackerRank question library
Response: Yes, HackerRank does support custom questions. In the HackerRank for Work platform, you can create your own custom coding challenges and add them to your question library. This allows you to write questions tailored to your specific tech stack, frameworks, or internal scenarios. Once created, these custom questions can be added to any test you build, just like questions from the standard library. You can access the custom question creation feature from within your company's HackerRank dashboard under the library or question management section.

Issue: Hello! I recently got a Visa credit card and I'm trying to understand the travel insurance benefits that come with it. I'm planning a trip to Southeast Asia and want to know if medical evacuation is covered, what the coverage limits are, and how I make a claim if something happens abroad.
Subject: Travel insurance benefits on my Visa credit card
Response: Visa credit cards can include a range of travel benefits, which may include travel accident insurance and emergency evacuation coverage depending on your specific card type and the issuing bank. However, the exact coverage limits and claim procedures are determined by your card-issuing bank, not Visa directly. We recommend contacting the customer service number on the back of your card or checking your card's benefits guide (often available through your bank's online portal) for the precise details of your travel insurance coverage and the claims process. You can also visit visa.com for general information about Visa card benefits.

Three things I want to raise: 1) The collaborative code editor sometimes lags badly when both the interviewer and candidate are typing simultaneously. 2) Is there a way to export interview recordings in MP4 format rather than just watching them in-platform? 3) We'd love a feature where we can create reusable interview 'templates' that preload specific questions and time limits so our interviewers don't have to configure everything from scratch each time.

Issue: My Visa card got declined at a restaurant last night which was super embarrassing. The card works fine for online purchases, I checked this morning. I also want to know if Visa offers any kind of purchase protection for items that get damaged after I buy them? And lastly, is there a way to set spending limits on my card?
Subject: Card declined + questions about purchase protection and spending limits
Response: Sorry to hear your card was declined — that can definitely be frustrating. For in-person declines that don't happen online, common causes include the merchant's terminal not reading the chip correctly, or a fraud flag triggered by an unusual location. We'd recommend contacting the number on the back of your card so an agent can review the decline reason on your account. Regarding purchase protection: many Visa cards include benefits like purchase security that covers eligible items against damage or theft for a limited time after purchase — the specifics depend on your card issuer, so check your card's benefits guide or call your issuing bank. Spending limit controls (like setting a daily cap) are typically managed through your card issuer's mobile app or by calling them directly, as Visa itself does not set individual card limits — your bank does.

Issue: Hi team, I have a few questions about HackerRank Screen. First, can I customize the time limit for individual questions within a test, or is it only at the overall test level? Second, do candidates see their score immediately after submitting, or is that controlled by the admin? Third, is there a way to prevent candidates from copy-pasting code into the editor?
Subject: HackerRank Screen - time limits, score visibility, copy-paste restriction
Response: Great questions about HackerRank Screen! Here's what you need to know: (1) Time limits can be set at the overall test level; individual question-level time limits are not a standard configuration option in most Screen plans, so the timer applies to the full assessment. (2) Score visibility after submission is controlled by the admin — you can configure whether candidates see their results or not from the test settings. (3) HackerRank Screen does have a plagiarism detection and proctoring feature that includes copy-paste monitoring; you can enable settings to flag or restrict copy-paste activity from outside the browser. Check your test's proctoring settings to turn this on. If you need specifics based on your plan, our support team can walk you through what's available on your account.

Issue: I'm traveling to Japan next month for work and I want to make sure my Visa card will work there. A couple of questions: Do I need to notify my bank before I travel? Will I get charged foreign transaction fees? And are there places in Japan that might not accept Visa?
Subject: Using Visa card in Japan - travel questions
Response: Great that you're planning ahead! Here's some guidance for using your Visa card in Japan: (1) Travel notice: It's generally a good idea to notify your card-issuing bank before international travel so they don't flag your transactions as suspicious and block the card — check your bank's app or call the number on the back of your card to set a travel notice. (2) Foreign transaction fees: These are set by your individual card issuer, not Visa directly. Review your card's terms or call your bank to find out if a foreign transaction fee applies and what the rate is. (3) Acceptance in Japan: Visa is widely accepted in Japan at major hotels, department stores, and larger retailers. However, Japan is still a cash-heavy country in many contexts — smaller restaurants, local shops, temples, and vending machines may be cash-only. It's advisable to carry some Japanese yen alongside your card.
